question: Does the image present a biological diagram of the human blood circulatory system, clearly displaying both systemic and pulmonary circulation pathways?
Does the heart section show a cross-sectional view with all four chambers clearly visible?
Are there text labels explicitly identifying the atrioventricular valves?
Are there text labels explicitly identifying the semilunar valves?
Are both the Superior Vena Cava and Inferior Vena Cava visibly present in the diagram?
Are capillary networks depicted for both the systemic and pulmonary circulation loops?
Is the systemic arterial pathway (Left Ventricle → Aorta → Branching Arteries → Capillary Network) marked with red color?
Is the systemic venous pathway (Capillary Network → Branching Veins → Vena Cava → Right Atrium) marked with blue color?
Is the pulmonary arterial pathway (Right Ventricle → Pulmonary Artery → Lung Capillaries) marked with blue color (indicating deoxygenated blood)?
Is the pulmonary venous pathway (Lung Capillaries → Pulmonary Veins → Left Atrium) marked with red color (indicating oxygenated blood)?
Are there visual arrows indicating the direction of blood flow throughout the pathways?
Are there text annotations on the side explaining the gas exchange process?
